current_executable_status: executable: code_ref resolves + test_ref present (status=implemented). Registry 'parameters' empty; defaults are labeled PRIORS, no validation history. code_ref=families.interaction:position_examination, formal P(examine|rank)=gamma^rank (default gamma=0.8).

primary_sources.title: An Experimental Comparison of Click Position-Bias Models
primary_sources.authors: Craswell, N.; Zoeter, O.; Taylor, M.; Ramsey, B.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Cascade/examination click models; shows lower-rank CTR is explained by an examination process that decays down a ranked list (structural click model).

primary_sources.title: Accurately Interpreting Clickthrough Data as Implicit Feedback
primary_sources.authors: Joachims, T.; Granka, L.; Pan, B.; Hembrooke, H.; Gay, G.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Eye-tracking evidence that users scan top-down and attention drops sharply below the top 1-2 ranks; motivates clicks as position-biased implicit feedback.

primary_sources.title: Unbiased Learning-to-Rank with Biased Feedback
primary_sources.authors: Joachims, T.; Swaminathan, A.; Schnabel, T.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Position-based propensity q_r=(1/r)^eta and inverse-propensity-scoring counterfactual LTR; propensities estimated by a swap/interventional experiment. PDF read directly.

formal_model: Examination hypothesis: P(click|rank,doc)=P(examine|rank)·P(relevant|doc). Decay forms: (a) Cascade (Craswell 2008): sequential top-down, P(examine r1)=1, P(examine r+1)=P(examine r)·(1-P(click at r)); (b) Position-Based propensity (Joachims 2017): q_r=(1/r)^eta, content-independent. Repo implements geometric variant P(examine|rank)=gamma^rank.
causal_assumptions: Separability: click = examination × relevance.
Examination depends only on rank/layout, not content (position-based model).
No trust/post-click bias in basic cascade.
Rank randomized/logged for propensity identification (swap/interventional).
evidence_type: structural_model (+ causal_estimate for propensity from swap experiments)

reported_coefficients.name: position_bias_severity_eta
reported_coefficients.context: q_r=(1/r)^eta; eta=1 default/estimated severity regime. Overestimating small propensities (eta<1) tolerable; underestimating (eta>1) harmful. Verified from PDF.

reported_coefficients.name: examination_probability_by_rank_eta1
reported_coefficients.context: Typical position-bias decay ranks 1-5 under eta=1 (rank10=0.1). The requested examination-probability-by-rank curve.

reported_coefficients.name: repo_examination_prior_gamma
reported_coefficients.context: P(examine|rank)=gamma^rank, gamma=0.8 is a LABELED PRIOR (not fitted); ranks0-4=1.0,0.8,0.64,0.512,0.41. Distinct from (1/r)^eta.

reported_coefficients.name: cascade_examination_decay_closed_form
reported_coefficients.context: Cascade decay is content-dependent (product of (1-click) above); no single universal rank curve / transportable point estimate.

datasets_in_literature: Search-engine click logs with logged/randomized rank (arXiv Full-Text Search, Joachims 2017)
replication_evidence: Position bias and top-down decay are among the most replicated IR findings (eye-tracking 2005; cascade/PBM/DBN 2008-2015; IPS-LTR 2017+). eta near 1 recurs but is platform-specific.
critiques_or_nulls: eta/gamma NOT transportable: severity depends on layout, device, vertical, presentation.
Cascade vs PBM vs DBN disagree at lower ranks; single-parameter decay is an approximation.
applicability_limits: Only where items are shown in an ordered list/feed and rank is observed.
Needs position variation to identify; fixed presentation gives no identification.
transport_risks: High: refit per platform/surface; web-search eta will not transfer to a social feed or grid.
Layout changes silently invalidate a fitted decay.
local_refit_feasible: NO. No local dataset varies/logs rank with clicks. Upworthy RANDOMIZES content (headline/image) but NOT position, so it identifies content-response/click-propensity, NOT position/examination bias. StackExchange answer order is not an examination log.
best_validation_route: External IPS/swap estimation on a rank-varying click log. Locally only the structural form is unit-testable; the decay parameter is a prior, unidentifiable from repo datasets.
expected_applicability: medium-high in ranked-feed/search domains; null in non-ranked settings.
planned_implementation: Keep structural family; expose both parameterizations (gamma^rank and (1/r)^eta); add IPS hook for an externally-estimated propensity vector per surface. Do NOT claim local validation.
promotion_blocker: No position-varying local click log -> examination parameter unidentifiable locally; cannot reach locally_validated. Upworthy explicitly does not identify position bias.

current_executable_status: executable: code_ref resolves + test_ref present (status=implemented). Registry 'parameters' empty; defaults are labeled PRIORS, no validation history. code_ref=families.interaction:rank_by_score (deterministic sort by score desc -> rank).

primary_sources.title: Unbiased Learning-to-Rank with Biased Feedback
primary_sources.authors: Joachims, T.; Swaminathan, A.; Schnabel, T.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Position-based propensity q_r=(1/r)^eta and inverse-propensity-scoring counterfactual LTR; propensities estimated by a swap/interventional experiment. PDF read directly.

primary_sources.title: An Experimental Comparison of Click Position-Bias Models
primary_sources.authors: Craswell, N.; Zoeter, O.; Taylor, M.; Ramsey, B.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Cascade/examination click models; shows lower-rank CTR is explained by an examination process that decays down a ranked list (structural click model).

formal_model: Attention/slot allocation by ranking: rank(i)=argsort_desc(score_i). Unbiased LTR minimizes IPS-weighted rank risk R_IPS=sum_{clicked i} rankloss/q_{rank(i)} (Joachims 2017). The sort is a deterministic order statistic (structural); learning the score is the fitted part.
causal_assumptions: Deterministic score->rank map (ties broken by rule).
(Learning) click propensities q_r known/estimated and positive (overlap).
Relevance is the intended allocation target.

reported_coefficients.name: ranking_operator_parameters
reported_coefficients.context: Sort has no free parameters; only the upstream scoring model is fitted. No transportable coefficient.

datasets_in_literature: LTR benchmarks: LETOR, MSLR-WEB10K/30K, Yahoo! LTR Challenge
Production click logs for counterfactual LTR (Joachims 2017)
replication_evidence: LTR and IPS-LTR are extensively benchmarked; the deterministic allocation step is analytic and needs no replication.
critiques_or_nulls: Only as good as the score it sorts; ranking asserts nothing causal.
Feedback loops: today's ranking shapes tomorrow's training clicks (position-bias confounding).
applicability_limits: Requires a defined per-item score and slotting/capacity structure.
Not behavioral; it is an allocation rule.
transport_risks: Low for the sort operator (universal); high for any fitted scoring model behind it.
local_refit_feasible: Operator is structural (no refit). A scorer could be fit on Upworthy CTR but that validates content-response, not the allocation rule.
best_validation_route: Structural correctness tests (order invariants, capacity). Optionally fit a CTR scorer on Upworthy to exercise the pipeline end-to-end.
planned_implementation: Retain as structural operator; document that only the scorer needs data; provide optional IPS-trained scorer pack tied to platform_examination propensities.
planned_parameter_packs: optional: Upworthy content-response scorer (identifies click-propensity, not ranking)
promotion_blocker: Structural operator has nothing to validate against data; promotion beyond software_implemented not meaningful without a paired validated scorer.

current_executable_status: executable: code_ref resolves + test_ref present (status=implemented). Registry 'parameters' empty; defaults are labeled PRIORS, no validation history. code_ref=transitions:InstitutionalVoteOperator (deterministic vote execution / tally).

primary_sources.title: A Spatial Model for Legislative Roll Call Analysis
primary_sources.what_it_provides: NOMINATE spatial-voting model; low-dimensional ideal-point model predicting legislator yea/nay (basis of DW-NOMINATE).

formal_model: Two layers: (a) deterministic aggregation R(votes)->outcome (majority/supermajority/weighted) — structural, exact. (b) Predictive spatial voting P(yea_ij)=F(-||x_i-O_yea||^2+||x_i-O_nay||^2) with ideal points x_i (NOMINATE/DW-NOMINATE), MLE-fit. Registry operator implements (a); (b) is a fittable overlay.
causal_assumptions: Rule execution deterministic and known.
(Spatial) single-peaked preferences in low-dimensional policy space; utility-maximizing votes with stochastic error.
Ideal points stable within window.

reported_coefficients.name: nominate_correct_classification_2d
reported_coefficients.source_ref: Poole & Rosenthal (NOMINATE); voteview DW-NOMINATE joint fit
reported_coefficients.context: 2-D spatial model reportedly classifies ~85% of individual roll-call choices; a voteview DW-NOMINATE joint fit of ~87.2% appeared in secondary summaries. Exact figure NOT confirmed from a primary page -> null.

reported_coefficients.name: aggregation_rule_params
reported_coefficients.context: Tally rule exact/deterministic; no free parameter.

replication_evidence: DW-NOMINATE ideal points re-estimated every Congress; low-dimensionality of US roll calls replicates across decades.
critiques_or_nulls: Spatial classification % is aggregate, inflated by lopsided votes; close votes harder.
applicability_limits: Tally rule needs the exact procedure (retrieved, not fabricated).
Spatial layer needs enough votes per member.
local_refit_feasible: PARTIAL. Local congress-votes dataset supports (i) validating the deterministic tally and (ii) fitting/holding-out a spatial-voting predictor. Tally itself is structural.
best_validation_route: Deterministic-tally unit tests on congress votes; held-out roll-call prediction accuracy for the optional spatial overlay.
expected_applicability: high for legislatures/committees/boards with explicit rules.
planned_implementation: Keep deterministic operator; add optional spatial-voting predictor validated on held-out congress roll calls (predictive overlay could reach locally_validated while rule stays structural).
promotion_blocker: Rule layer structural (nothing to validate). Predictive overlay has no held-out record yet; needs a hold-out roll-call evaluation to advance.

family: Veto / quorum / agenda control / stage transitions
current_executable_status: executable: code_ref resolves + test_ref present (status=implemented). Registry 'parameters' empty; defaults are labeled PRIORS, no validation history. code_ref=institutions:Rule (procedure rule gates actions by stage variable).

primary_sources.title: Political Resource Allocation, Controlled Agendas, and the Status Quo
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Agenda-setter model: a monopoly proposer facing a reversion (status-quo) point extracts outcomes far from the median; formal comparative statics of gatekeeping/agenda control.

formal_model: Rule-based gatekeeping/stage machine: an action is admissible only if stage/quorum/veto predicates hold; transitions deterministic given inputs. Game-theoretic backing (Romer-Rosenthal 1978): setter proposes x, voters compare to reversion q; equilibrium can sit far from the median, bounded by setter monopoly and status-quo. Structural/analytic — institutional constants, not fitted.
causal_assumptions: Procedure (proposers, thresholds, veto points, quorum) known and correctly encoded.
Reversion/status-quo well-defined.
(Setter) monopoly agenda power; sincere voting on take-it-or-leave-it offer.

reported_coefficients.name: agenda_setter_outcome_bound
reported_coefficients.context: Comparative-statics result (outcome pinned by setter ideal point vs reversion), not a numeric coefficient; no transportable point estimate.

replication_evidence: Agenda-setting/gatekeeping is foundational and repeatedly formalized; setter-power effects documented in budget-referendum studies. Analytic, not statistically fitted.
critiques_or_nulls: Hinges on the often-unobserved reversion point and monopoly-proposer assumption.
Real procedures allow amendments/multiple veto players, weakening the clean result.
applicability_limits: Requires the actual procedure retrieved and encoded correctly.
transport_risks: Low as a mechanism form; specific rules must be re-specified per institution (retrieval risk, not statistical transport risk).
local_refit_feasible: N/A (structural). No fitting; validate by rule-execution correctness. Congress data can sanity-check quorum/threshold gating.
best_validation_route: Scenario/rule-execution tests: verify gating, quorum, veto, stage transitions on constructed procedural traces.
expected_applicability: high wherever explicit procedure governs action admissibility.
planned_implementation: Keep as structural rule engine; add a library of encoded procedures (quorum, supermajority, veto, agenda-setter) with correctness tests. No parameter fitting.
promotion_blocker: Structural family: no statistical validation target, so stays software_implemented by design; 'validation' = procedure-encoding correctness, not held-out fit.

current_executable_status: not_implemented: no code_ref, no registry record, no test_ref.

primary_sources.title: Cooperation and Punishment in Public Goods Experiments
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Public-goods game (n=4, 20 tokens, MPCR a=0.4, 10 periods, Partner/Stranger x punish/no-punish). Extracted final-period means and punishment technology (PDF read directly).

primary_sources.title: Altruistic Punishment in Humans
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Perfect-stranger public-goods experiment (n=4, 20 MU, 6 periods): cooperation high/sustained with costly punishment, collapses toward free-riding without.

primary_sources.title: Crime and Punishment: An Economic Approach
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Structural deterrence model: offenses fall in apprehension probability p and severity f; supply-of-offenses O(p,f,u) as a rational cost-benefit response.

formal_model: (a) Becker (1968) deterrence: offenses O=O(p,f,u) decreasing in apprehension probability p and sanction severity f; optimal enforcement trades cost vs harm. (b) Fehr-Gächter costly/altruistic punishment: players pay c(p) to cut a target's payoff; credible punishment threat sustains cooperation. contribution_{t+1} rises with expected punishment for below-average contribution.
causal_assumptions: (Becker) rational expected-utility offenders respond to p and f.
(Fehr-Gächter) punishment costly to punisher and materially non-beneficial (identifies norm-enforcing motive); RANDOMIZED punish/no-punish treatment gives causal identification.
Perfect-stranger design (FG2002) removes reputation/repeated-game confounds.
required_variables: punishment cost schedule c(p) and impact
temporal_scale: event-to-episodic (per-round punishment; deterrence over repeated interaction)

reported_coefficients.name: final_period_contribution_with_punishment
reported_coefficients.source_ref: Fehr & Gächter 2000 AER (10.1257/aer.90.4.980), Partner final period
reported_coefficients.context: Out of 20-token endowment; converges toward full cooperation. Verified from PDF text.

reported_coefficients.name: final_period_contribution_no_punishment
reported_coefficients.source_ref: Fehr & Gächter 2000 AER, Partner final period
reported_coefficients.context: Out of 20; decays toward free-riding without punishment. Verified from PDF text.

reported_coefficients.name: punishment_vs_nopunishment_ratio_stranger
reported_coefficients.source_ref: Fehr & Gächter 2000 AER, Stranger, mean over 10 periods
reported_coefficients.context: Contributions 2-4x higher with punishment; significant at all conventional levels. Verified from PDF.

reported_coefficients.name: punishment_impact_per_point
reported_coefficients.context: Each punishment point reduces target's first-stage payoff by 10%; cost schedule c(p)=[0,1,2,4,6,9,12,16,20,25,30] for p=0..10. Verified from PDF.

reported_coefficients.name: sustained_cooperation_range_with_punishment
reported_coefficients.context: With punishment, average contribution rates of 50-95% of endowment maintained. Verified from PDF.

reported_coefficients.name: design_params
reported_coefficients.context: Group size, endowment (tokens), MPCR, periods. Verified from PDF.

reported_coefficients.name: fg2002_nature_figure2_point_values
reported_coefficients.context: Exact per-period Figure-2 means (perfect-stranger, 6 periods) not machine-readable from the primary Nature PDF (blocked); qualitative result verified, point values not.

reported_coefficients.name: becker_deterrence_elasticity
reported_coefficients.context: Theory paper gives signs/structure (dO/dp<0, dO/df<0), not fitted elasticities; certainty>severity is from later empirical work, not this source.

datasets_in_literature: Public-goods-with-punishment lab experiments (Fehr-Gächter; cross-cultural Herrmann/Thöni/Gächter 2008 Science)
replication_evidence: Costly-punishment-sustains-cooperation replicates across labs/cultures (with antisocial-punishment variation). Certainty>severity deterrence is a robust criminology finding.
critiques_or_nulls: Altruistic punishment can reduce net group welfare (early-sequence relative-gain losses).
Cross-cultural 'antisocial punishment' of high contributors undermines the clean result in some societies.
Becker elasticities are not in the original theory paper; must not be fabricated.
applicability_limits: Lab public-goods magnitudes may not transport to field enforcement.
Needs a defined punishment technology (cost+impact) and observable norm deviations.
transport_risks: High: effectiveness depends on cost:impact ratio, anonymity, culture, repeated-vs-one-shot.
local_refit_feasible: NO local dataset has a punishment/enforcement treatment (Upworthy/congress/StackExchange lack costly-punishment or deviation-punishment structure). Refit needs public-goods-with-punishment data.
best_validation_route: Implement the family, then validate costly-punishment against Fehr-Gächter-style experimental data (external); for deterrence, calibrate O(p,f) signs against a crime panel. No local route.
priority: HIGH (registry gap with the strongest verified numeric evidence in this cluster).
planned_implementation: Add 'enforcement' family with two operators: (i) Becker deterrence O(p,f); (ii) costly-punishment update contribution_{t+1}=g(deviation, expected_punishment) with the FG cost schedule as a labeled pack. Ship with external-validation record only (honest: no local data).
planned_parameter_packs: fehr_gachter_2000_pack: impact=0.10/point, c(p)=[0,1,2,4,6,9,12,16,20,25,30], n=4, endow=20, a=0.4 (VERIFIED)
promotion_blocker: Not implemented (no code_ref/test_ref) AND no local dataset to identify enforcement parameters -> cannot exceed structural_candidate_only until built + externally validated.

current_executable_status: resource_depletion executable (code_ref=transitions:ResourceUpdateOperator, conservation-checked consumption). capacity/queueing NOT implemented as a mechanism family (EventQueue classes in events.py/simulation are discrete-event ENGINE plumbing, not a fitted queue model).

primary_sources.title: A Proof for the Queuing Formula: L = λW
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Little's Law L=λW (mean number in system = arrival rate × mean sojourn); distribution-free analytic queueing identity underpinning capacity/queue models.

formal_model: (a) Resource depletion: conservation accounting r_{t+1}=r_t - consumption + replenishment with non-negativity/conservation checks — structural. (b) Queueing/capacity: Little's Law L=λW; M/M/1 stationary results (utilization ρ=λ/μ, mean wait W=1/(μ-λ)) — analytic; Little's Law distribution-free. Parameters (λ,μ,capacity) measured, not regression-fit.
causal_assumptions: Conservation: resource neither created nor destroyed except by modeled flows.
(Little) system stable (arrivals=departures long-run), stationary over averaging window.
(M/M/1) Poisson arrivals, exponential service, single server, FIFO, infinite buffer for closed forms.
required_variables: arrival rate λ, service rate μ, server/slot count, buffer/capacity

reported_coefficients.name: littles_law
reported_coefficients.context: Exact distribution-free identity; no free parameter — λ and W are measured. Verified citation.

reported_coefficients.name: mm1_mean_number_in_system
reported_coefficients.context: Analytic result for Poisson/exponential single-server queue; structural, not fitted.

reported_coefficients.name: resource_conservation_params
reported_coefficients.context: Depletion operator has no free parameter beyond measured flow rates.

replication_evidence: Little's Law and M/M/1 are mathematically proven, not empirically replicated; hold by derivation. Empirical use = plug measured λ, μ, W.
critiques_or_nulls: Closed-form M/M/1 fails under bursty (non-Poisson) arrivals, heavy tails, or feedback; Little's Law still holds with correct stationary averages.
Depletion accounting says nothing about the behavioral demand generating consumption.
applicability_limits: Queue closed-forms need stability (ρ<1) and distributional assumptions; else use Little's Law only or simulate.
Capacity as a mechanism must be added; currently absent from the registry.
transport_risks: Low for analytic identities (universal); parameters (λ,μ) are setting-specific and measured, not transported.
local_refit_feasible: PARTIAL. StackExchange answer-arrival is queue-like (response-occurrence timing) and supports DESCRIPTIVE arrival/service-rate estimation and Little's-Law bookkeeping. Depletion is structural (unit-test conservation). Not causal identification.
best_validation_route: Structural: conservation unit tests for depletion; simulate M/M/1 and verify L=λW. Descriptive: estimate arrival/service rates on StackExchange answer timings and check Little's-Law consistency.
expected_applicability: high wherever throughput/capacity/backlog matter (moderation queues, support, attention budgets).
priority: medium (add explicit capacity/queueing family; depletion already covered).
planned_implementation: Add a 'capacity_queue' structural family exposing Little's Law + M/M/1 / M/M/c closed forms and a stability guard; keep resource_depletion as the conservation operator. Parameters measured per setting (labeled measured, not fitted).
planned_parameter_packs: structural_queue: Little's Law + M/M/1 closed forms (no fitted params)
promotion_blocker: Analytic families have no held-out statistical target; capacity/queueing also not yet implemented. Depletion stays software_implemented; queueing needs building (structural_candidate) first.

current_executable_status: executable: code_ref resolves + test_ref present (status=implemented). Registry 'parameters' empty; defaults are labeled PRIORS, no validation history. code_ref=transitions:RareEventArrivalOperator (Poisson rare-event arrival by deadline).

primary_sources.title: A New Approach to the Economic Analysis of Nonstationary Time Series and the Business Cycle
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Markov regime-switching model: discrete unobserved state governs the DGP; MLE + filter/smoother to infer regime probabilities and transition matrix.

formal_model: (a) Rare-event arrival: Poisson process N(t)~Poisson(λt); P(>=1 by T)=1-e^{-λT} — structural. (b) Regime switching (Hamilton 1989): latent state s_t follows a Markov chain with transition matrix P; y_t=μ_{s_t}+ε_t; regimes inferred by MLE + Hamilton filter/smoother — FITTABLE (methodological), unlike Poisson arrival.
causal_assumptions: (Poisson) memoryless, constant-rate, independent arrivals over the window.
(Regime-switching) finite hidden states; Markov transitions; parameters constant within regime; correct state count K.

reported_coefficients.name: poisson_arrival_form
reported_coefficients.context: λ measured per setting; no universal value.

reported_coefficients.name: regime_switching_estimator
reported_coefficients.context: Provides MLE/filter for a 2-state Markov-switching model of US GNP growth; transition probabilities and state means are dataset-specific (no transportable point estimate).

replication_evidence: Markov-switching is a workhorse of nonlinear time-series econometrics (thousands of applications); Poisson arrival is textbook structural. Both established methodologically rather than as a single replicated effect size.
critiques_or_nulls: Regime count K and labeling are researcher choices; identification issues with many states.
Poisson constant-rate fails for clustered/self-exciting shocks (repo already PRESERVES a Hawkes self-excitation FAILURE vs Poisson — do not overclaim Poisson).
Regime parameters series-specific; importing another study's transition matrix is a transport error.
applicability_limits: Poisson arrival for genuinely rare, independent shocks only.
Regime-switching needs a long-enough series to identify states.
transport_risks: High for fitted regime parameters; medium for assumed arrival rates.
local_refit_feasible: LIMITED. No clean local regime/shock series among Upworthy/congress/StackExchange; Poisson arrival is structural (calibrate λ where an arrival stream exists, e.g., StackExchange). Regime-switching needs an external macro/finance series.
best_validation_route: Structural: verify Poisson arrival calibration and deadline probabilities. Methodological: fit a 2-state Markov-switching model on an external macro/finance series and check out-of-sample regime recovery. Preserve the existing Hawkes-vs-Poisson failure note.
expected_applicability: medium (shocks/regimes common but data-hungry to identify).
priority: low-medium (Poisson done; regime-switching is an optional fittable add).
planned_implementation: Keep Poisson operator; optionally add a Markov-switching operator (Hamilton filter) with parameters fit per external series and clearly labeled non-transportable. Carry forward the Hawkes-failure caveat.
promotion_blocker: Poisson layer structural (no held-out target); regime-switching not implemented and has no local identifying series -> stays software_implemented until a regime operator is built + externally validated.
